finding: B. ACQUIRED CYST (a) centrilobular / bullous emphysema

differentials.name: Bleb = cystic air collection within visceral pleura; mostly apical with narrow neck
differentials.raw: Bleb = cystic air collection within visceral pleura; mostly apical with narrow neck

differentials.name: Bulla = sharply demarcated dilated air space within lung parenchyma > 1 cm in diameter with epithelialized wall < 1 mm thick due to destruction of alveoli
differentials.raw: Bulla = sharply demarcated dilated air space within lung parenchyma > 1 cm in diameter with epithelialized wall < 1 mm thick due to destruction of alveoli (= air cyst in localized / centrilobular / panlobular emphysema)

differentials.name: "Vanishing lung" = large area of localized emphysema causing atelectasis + dyspnea
differentials.raw: "Vanishing lung" = large area of localized emphysema causing atelectasis + dyspnea

differentials.name: Traumatic pneumatocele: lung hematoma / hydrocarbon inhalation (c)  cystic bronchiectasis
differentials.raw: Traumatic pneumatocele: lung hematoma / hydrocarbon inhalation (c)  cystic bronchiectasis

differentials.name: Cystic fibrosis
differentials.raw: Cystic fibrosis (more obvious in upper lobes)

context: √  typically at lung apex √  slow progressive enlargement Cx: Rx:   surgical resection if bulla > ⅓ of hemithorax (b)  pneumatocele √  multiple thin-walled lucencies with air-fluid levels in lower lobes √  involvement of proximal perihilar bronchi
